basics.name: Gustavo Lahr
basics.summary: Assistant Professor in the Biomedical Engineering program at Hospital Israelita Albert Einstein and researcher at its Brain Institute. His research focuses on robotic rehabilitation and assistive technologies, with an emphasis on wearable sensors, artificial intelligence, and humanrobot interaction, particularly in clinical contexts such as neurological rehabilitation. He completed a postdoctoral fellowship in robotics at the Human-Robot Interfaces and Interaction Laboratory (HRII), Istituto Italiano di Tecnologia, Italy, working on dynamic manipulation, adaptive interaction control, and humanrobot interaction under the Leonardo@IIT project. He holds a Ph.D. and M.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering from the São Carlos School of Engineering, University of São Paulo (EESC-USP), in the field of artificial intelligence applied to dynamic tasks using robotic manipulators. He earned his B.Sc. in Mechatronic Engineering from EESC-USP. During his doctoral studies, he was a visiting researcher through the CAPES Doctoral Sandwich Program (PDSE) at KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Sweden, in the Robotics, Perception and Learning Laboratory. His research interests include robotic manipulation, brainmachine interfaces, biomechatronics, and intelligent systems applied to healthcare.

work.company: Faculdade Israelita de Ciências da Saúde Albert Einstein (FICSAE)
work.summary: Assistant Professor in the Biomedical Engineering undergraduate program. Courses taught: Numerical Calculus, Analog Electronics, Fundamentals of Design and Manufacturing, Signals and Systems. Coordinator of the Internationalization Center of Biomedical Engineering (since 2026).

work.company: Instituto Israelita de Ensino e Pesquisa Albert Einstein (IIEPAE)
work.summary: Researcher at the Brain Institute of Hospital Israelita Albert Einstein. Research line: Applications of collaborative robots in healthcare. Also teaches Collaborative Robotics in the specialization program in Applied Bioengineering in Health.

Postdoctoral fellowship at the Human-Robot Interfaces and Interaction Laboratory (HRII). Research on dynamic manipulation, adaptive interaction control, and human-robot interaction under the Leonardo@IIT project.
Substitute professor for Aircraft Control Systems I and Machine Elements.
Part-time assistant professor in Mechanical Engineering.

Industrial automation: programming KUKA and MOTOMAN robots for pick-and-place tasks, mechanical design of a micro-parts pick-and-place machine, and calibration of a computer-vision metrology system (Sherlock software).

education.thesis: Robotic assembly of threaded fasteners: controller optimization and failure detection with machine learning
Real-time motion synthesis for dynamic precision assembly tasks using robots
Instrumentation and planning of the control system for a power-assisted wheelchair.
